2604.20175.title: Physics-Enhanced Deep Learning for Proactive Thermal Runaway Forecasting in Li-Ion Batteries
2604.20175.paper_summary: Accurate prediction of thermal runaway in lithium-ion batteries is essential for ensuring the safety, efficiency, and reliability of modern energy storage systems. Conventional data-driven approaches, such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, can capture complex temporal dependencies but often violate thermodynamic principles, resulting in physically inconsistent predictions. Conversely, physics-based thermal models provide interpretability but are computationally expensive and difficult to parameterize for real-time applications. To bridge this gap, this study proposes a Physics-Informed Long Short-Term Memory (PI-LSTM) framework that integrates governing heat transfer equations directly into the deep learning architecture through a physics-based regularization term in the loss function. The model leverages multi-feature input sequences, including state of charge, voltage, current, mechanical stress, and surface temperature, to forecast battery temperature evolution while enforcing thermal diffusion constraints. Extensive experiments conducted on thirteen lithium-ion battery datasets demonstrate that the proposed PI-LSTM achieves an 81.9% reduction in root mean square error (RMSE) and an 81.3% reduction in mean absolute error (MAE) compared to the standard LSTM baseline, while also outperforming CNN-LSTM and multilayer perceptron (MLP) models by wide margins. The inclusion of physical constraints enhances the model's generalization across diverse operating conditions and eliminates non-physical temperature oscillations. These results confirm that physics-informed deep learning offers a viable pathway toward interpretable, accurate, and real-time thermal management in next-generation battery systems.
2604.20175.paper_summary_zh: 準確預測鋰離子電池的熱失控對於確保現代能源儲存系統的安全性、效率和可靠性至關重要。傳統的數據驅動方法，如長短期記憶（LSTM）網絡，能夠捕捉複雜的時間依賴性，但常常違反熱力學原則，導致物理上不一致的預測。相反，基於物理的熱模型提供了解釋性，但在計算上成本高昂，且對於實時應用難以參數化。為了彌補這一差距，本研究提出了一種物理知識驅動的長短期記憶（PI-LSTM）框架，通過在損失函數中引入基於物理的正則化項，將控制熱傳遞方程直接整合到深度學習架構中。該模型利用多特徵輸入序列，包括充電狀態、電壓、電流、機械應力和表面溫度，來預測電池溫度的演變，同時強制執行熱擴散約束。在十三個鋰離子電池數據集上進行的大量實驗表明，所提出的PI-LSTM相較於標準LSTM基準，實現了81.9%的均方根誤差（RMSE）減少和81.3%的平均絕對誤差（MAE）減少，同時在性能上也大幅超越了CNN-LSTM和多層感知器（MLP）模型。物理約束的納入增強了模型在多樣化操作條件下的泛化能力，並消除了非物理的溫度振蕩。這些結果證實，物理知識驅動的深度學習為下一代電池系統的可解釋、準確和實時熱管理提供了一條可行的途徑。
2604.20175.author: Salman Khan et.al.
2604.20175.authors: Salman Khan, Muhammad Zunair Zamir, Syed Sajid Ullah, Jie Li
